title: Developing AI Apps and Agents on Azure
passing_note: AI-103 passes at 700/1000. Microsoft does not publish the raw-score mapping, so the readiness estimate below is unofficial and based on percent correct only.

readiness_bands.label: Ready
readiness_bands.note: Consistently strong — you're tracking well above the pass line.

readiness_bands.label: Approaching
readiness_bands.note: Close — shore up weaker domains before test day.

readiness_bands.label: Not ready yet
readiness_bands.note: Keep studying — focus on your lowest-scoring domains.

scenarios.s-rag: Northwind Health is building an internal chat assistant that answers employee questions from a large library of HR and benefits PDFs held in Azure Blob Storage. Leadership wants grounded answers with source citations, a low rate of fabricated content, and a guarantee that all conversation history remains inside the company's own Azure subscription for compliance.
scenarios.s-agents: Contoso Travel is building a multi-agent concierge. A primary coordinating agent delegates to specialized sub-agents that look up flight prices, check hotel availability, and send booking confirmation emails to customers. Some of these actions are irreversible and must never execute without an explicit human check.
scenarios.s-docpipe: Fabrikam Insurance receives scanned claim packets that mix typed forms, handwritten notes, embedded photographs, and short video clips. The team needs an automated pipeline that reads the documents, extracts structured fields with source citations, describes the visual content, and indexes everything so a downstream retrieval agent can answer adjuster questions.
scenarios.s-govern: Adatum is rolling out a customer-facing generative assistant across several regions and must pass a responsible-AI review board. The board requires content filtering, defense against prompt injection, complete trace-based auditing, ongoing quality monitoring in production, and least-privilege access with no API keys stored in application code.

domains.title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A team must run inference entirely on a battery-powered sensor with 2 GB of RAM and no network connection. Which model category fits this constraint?

options.text: A frontier reasoning model such as an o-series model
A small language model such as Phi-4 Mini Instruct
A large multimodal model such as GPT-4o
A Model Router deployment

questions.explanation: Small language models are designed for on-device and edge scenarios with tight compute budgets, making an SLM the only realistic fit for severely constrained hardware. Frontier, multimodal, and router deployments all assume far more compute or connectivity than the sensor provides.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: An application serves users whose query complexity varies widely and wants cheaper models used automatically for simple prompts, all behind one endpoint with no client-side selection logic. What should be deployed?

options.text: A separate deployment for every candidate model, chosen in code
An embedding model deployment
A Model Router deployment
A managed compute endpoint for a fine-tuned model

questions.explanation: A Model Router deployment dispatches each incoming prompt at runtime to the most cost-effective capable model and exposes a single Chat Completions endpoint, so the caller does not implement routing logic. The other options require manual selection or serve a single fixed model.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Which model choices are appropriate for edge or resource-constrained deployments? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Phi-4 Mini Instruct
Meta Llama 1B/3B
An o-series extended-reasoning model for the same edge device
A frontier GPT-5 model running on the device itself

questions.explanation: Phi-4 Mini Instruct and small Llama variants are built for on-device and edge inference. o-series reasoning models trade latency for depth and are not edge-optimized, and a frontier GPT-5 model cannot run within tight on-device compute budgets.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A legal team needs an agent that answers questions grounded in contract libraries stored in the organization's internal SharePoint. Which Foundry capability should be configured?

options.text: Grounding with Bing Search
The SharePoint tool in the Foundry Agent Service
The Fabric Data Agent
The Code Interpreter tool

questions.explanation: The SharePoint tool connects an agent directly to organizational SharePoint document libraries without a manual export or indexing step. Bing grounding targets live public web content, the Fabric Data Agent targets structured Fabric data, and Code Interpreter runs sandboxed code.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A solution must classify user-uploaded images for harmful content before they are stored. Which Foundry capability provides this?

options.text: Azure AI Search
Azure AI Content Safety
Foundry IQ
The Responses API

questions.explanation: Azure AI Content Safety performs real-time harm classification for both text and images and can act as a guardrail on the input path. Search and Foundry IQ handle retrieval, and the Responses API orchestrates agent runs rather than moderating content.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Azure AI Search is the underlying retrieval engine for both Foundry IQ and the agent File Search tool.
questions.explanation: Azure AI Search provides the indexing and retrieval layer that both Foundry IQ and the File Search tool are built on, which is why index configuration decisions affect both features.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: After a RAG deployment, keyword searches return accurate results but semantic, intent-based queries perform poorly. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The index uses too many replicas
The query-time vectorizer does not use the same embedding model as the indexing skillset
Semantic ranking is scoring too many candidates
The indexer schedule interval is too short

questions.explanation: Cosine similarity is only meaningful when the query and document vectors come from the same embedding model with matching dimensions. A vectorizer that differs from the indexing model silently degrades vector relevance while leaving keyword matching intact.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A team wants the highest combined recall and precision for a RAG retrieval step without manually choosing keyword or vector mode per query. Which configuration should they use?

options.text: BM25 keyword search only
Pure vector search only
Hybrid search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion plus semantic ranking
Exhaustive KNN over the full index for every query

questions.explanation: Hybrid search fuses BM25 and vector results with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and semantic ranking then re-scores the top candidates by intent, giving the best overall relevance for most RAG workloads. Single-mode retrieval trades off recall or precision.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Which statements about integrated vectorization in Azure AI Search are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: It requires an indexer and cannot be used with the direct push API
A skillset splits text and calls an embedding model during indexing
The query-time vectorizer must use the same embedding model as the skillset
It removes the need for any vector field in the index schema

questions.explanation: Integrated vectorization runs inside an indexer pipeline where a skillset chunks and embeds content, and the query-time vectorizer must match the indexing embedding model. A vector field is still required in the index schema to store the embeddings.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: An agent must recall a user's stated dietary preferences across separate chat sessions that occur days apart. Which capability meets this need?

options.text: In-thread short-term memory
The Foundry Agent Service Memory Store
A larger model context window
The Code Interpreter tool

questions.explanation: Short-term memory lives only within an active thread and is lost when the session ends. The Memory Store persists extracted knowledge such as preferences across sessions and is consulted at the start of each new run.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A single agent run must search two separate Azure AI Search indexes. Because only one File Search tool instance is allowed per run, how should the solution be designed?

options.text: Attach two File Search tools to the same agent
Merge both indexes into one at query time inside the model prompt
Use connected agents, giving each sub-agent its own File Search tool pointed at one index
Increase the run timeout so both indexes can be scanned sequentially

questions.explanation: The run limit of one instance per knowledge tool type means a single agent cannot hold two File Search tools. Connected agents let a primary agent delegate to sub-agents, each carrying its own File Search tool bound to a different index.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: In the Northwind Health scenario, compliance requires that every agent conversation thread stay inside the company's own subscription. Which agent setup satisfies this?

options.text: Basic Agent Setup with Microsoft-managed storage
Standard Agent Setup with a customer-owned Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL thread store
Any setup, because thread data is always customer-owned
A larger context window so history is never persisted

questions.explanation: Standard Agent Setup routes conversation thread data through customer-provided resources such as Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL, keeping it inside the customer subscription. Basic Agent Setup stores threads in Microsoft-managed multitenant storage.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A solution needs customer-managed keys (CMK) for a Foundry resource. Which Key Vault requirement must be satisfied?

options.text: The Key Vault must be in any region as long as it is in the same subscription
The Key Vault must be in the same region and have soft delete and purge protection enabled
The Key Vault must disable soft delete to allow key rotation
A separate Key Vault is required for every project in the resource

questions.explanation: CMK requires a Key Vault in the same region as the Foundry resource with soft delete and purge protection enabled, and the resource's managed identity granted Key Vault Crypto User. One Key Vault covers secrets for all projects in the resource.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A developer should be able to build and manage agents inside one Foundry project but must not gain data-plane access to sibling projects in the same resource. What assignment achieves this?

options.text: Foundry User at the resource scope
Foundry Account Owner at the resource scope
Foundry User at the individual project scope
Owner at the subscription scope

questions.explanation: A role assignment scoped to a single project restricts data-plane access to that project only. Assigning at the resource scope would apply across all projects, and broader roles grant far more than needed.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A retailer generates one million product descriptions overnight with no real-time latency requirement and wants the lowest per-token cost. Which deployment type fits?

options.text: Global Standard
Global Provisioned (PTU)
Global Batch
Regional Standard

questions.explanation: Global Batch offers the cheapest per-token rate for asynchronous bulk processing and has no latency SLA, which suits an overnight, non-interactive workload. The standard and provisioned options are priced for interactive traffic.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: An enterprise wants to run a partner model such as Llama with pay-per-token pricing and no dedicated compute to manage. Which deployment option is correct?

options.text: Managed Compute
Serverless API
Global Provisioned (PTU)
Regional Provisioned (PTU)

questions.explanation: Serverless API provides pay-as-you-go access to partner and community models without provisioning compute. Managed Compute requires a dedicated endpoint, and provisioned options reserve capacity billed hourly.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Which limitations apply to Serverless API deployments compared with Standard or Foundry-resource deployments? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: They do not support keyless Entra ID authentication
They do not support custom content filters
They require a provisioned PTU reservation
They operate under third-party terms for partner and community models

questions.explanation: Serverless API endpoints use API keys rather than keyless Entra ID auth, do not support custom content filters, and serve partner or community models under third-party terms. They are pay-per-token and do not require a PTU reservation.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: An agent classifies documents into fixed categories and must return the same category for the same input every time. Which generation setting best supports this?

options.text: temperature set to 1.0
temperature set to 0.0
A higher max_tokens value
top_p set to 0.5 while also raising temperature

questions.explanation: A temperature of 0.0 maximizes determinism, which is ideal for classification and structured-output tasks. Raising temperature or combining it with top_p adjustments increases variability.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: When both an agent and its underlying model deployment have a guardrail assigned, the agent's guardrail fully overrides the model's guardrail for all traffic through that agent.
questions.explanation: Foundry gives the agent-level guardrail complete precedence over the model deployment's guardrail, so any scanning of tool calls and responses must be configured in the agent's guardrail.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A team wants to deploy Foundry agents from GitHub Actions without storing any long-lived Azure credentials in repository secrets. Which authentication approach should they use?

options.text: A stored client secret referenced by the workflow
OpenID Connect with a federated Entra credential
A personal access token for the deploying developer
A shared account key committed to the repository

questions.explanation: OpenID Connect lets the pipeline exchange a short-lived GitHub-issued token for an Azure access token through a federated credential, so no long-lived secret is stored. Stored secrets and keys are exactly what OIDC is meant to eliminate.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A pipeline fails to deploy a hosted agent container. The pipeline identity holds only the Foundry User role on the project. What additional role is most likely required?

options.text: Cognitive Services Usages Reader at subscription scope
Contributor on the project (plus ACR roles for image push)
Reader on the resource group
Key Vault Crypto User on the connected Key Vault

questions.explanation: Foundry User grants data-plane access for running agents but not the management-plane rights to create or update hosted agent versions. Code deployment mode also needs Contributor on the project plus container registry roles.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A subscription holds approved PTU quota in a region, but creating a new provisioned deployment fails. What is the most likely explanation?

options.text: The subscription exceeded its number of pay-per-token deployments
Physical capacity for that deployment type is unavailable even though quota exists
TPM and PTU quotas share a single pool that is now empty
Model Router must be enabled before any provisioned deployment

questions.explanation: PTU quota is a policy limit with no charge; actual compute capacity must also be available at deployment time. Having quota does not guarantee that capacity exists, so a deployment can still fail.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Once PTU quota is approved for a region, a provisioned deployment in that region is guaranteed to succeed.
questions.explanation: PTU quota is only a policy limit. The physical compute capacity must also be available at deployment time, so approved quota does not guarantee that a provisioned deployment will succeed.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A team wants to confirm that production agent responses are grounded in retrieved context without consuming generative model token quota. Which evaluator fits?

options.text: The standard Groundedness evaluator
Groundedness Pro, which uses Azure AI Content Safety
The Fluency evaluator
The Relevance evaluator

questions.explanation: Groundedness Pro returns a binary pass/fail using Azure AI Content Safety and requires no generative model deployment, so it does not consume model token quota. The standard Groundedness evaluator uses a judge model, and Fluency and Relevance measure different properties.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: During integrated vectorization, an indexer run reports partial success and some documents are missing. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The search service ran out of replicas
The Azure OpenAI embedding model quota was exhausted, so embedding calls for some documents failed
Semantic ranking was disabled on the index
The schedule interval was set below five minutes

questions.explanation: Integrated vectorization calls the embedding API for each chunk. When embedding quota is exhausted mid-run, those calls fail and the affected documents are skipped while others succeed, producing a partial-success result.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: An Azure Function with a system-assigned managed identity must call a Foundry endpoint without storing any API key. Which approach is recommended?

options.text: Read an API key from application settings
Use DefaultAzureCredential, which uses the managed identity when running in Azure
Embed a shared secret in the deployment package
Use a Serverless API deployment with a stored key

questions.explanation: DefaultAzureCredential automatically uses the managed identity when the code runs in Azure and a developer credential locally, so no key is stored in code or configuration. The other options all rely on stored secrets.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: In the Adatum scenario, an agent must block harmful content returned by a web search tool before that content reaches the model. Which guardrail intervention point must be configured?

options.text: User Input
Output
Tool Response
Model auto-update

questions.explanation: The Tool Response intervention point scans content returned from a tool to the agent before the agent processes it, blocking poisoned or harmful retrieved content. User Input scans the prompt and Output scans the final completion.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Which guardrail intervention points apply only to agents and not to standalone model deployments? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Tool Call
Tool Response
User Input
Output

questions.explanation: Tool Call and Tool Response are agent-only intervention points that scan the agent's planned actions and the content returned to it. User Input and Output apply to both model deployments and agents.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: Which evaluators require a reference ground-truth answer to produce a meaningful score? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: ROUGE
BLEU
Groundedness Pro
Coherence

questions.explanation: Textual similarity evaluators such as ROUGE and BLEU measure n-gram overlap against a reference answer, so they require ground truth. Groundedness Pro judges against retrieved context and Coherence judges internal logical flow, neither of which needs a reference.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A regulated solution must produce an audit trail linking each agent output to the exact tool calls and retrieved documents that produced it. Which foundation provides this in Foundry?

options.text: OpenTelemetry traces stored in the connected Application Insights instance
Increasing the model context window
A larger PTU reservation
Enabling Model Router

questions.explanation: Foundry tracing is built on OpenTelemetry, storing spans in the connected Application Insights instance. Each trace links an output to the tool calls, retrieved documents, and model invocations that produced it, providing lineage for auditing.
questions.domain_title: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution

questions.stem: A team fine-tuned a model on proprietary data and needs a dedicated endpoint with predictable throughput. Which deployment type is required?

options.text: Serverless API
Managed Compute
Global Batch
Model Router

questions.explanation: Serverless API deployments cannot host custom fine-tuned weights. Managed Compute runs the model on a dedicated container endpoint that hosts custom weights and delivers consistent throughput through Azure Machine Learning scaling rules.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about inference SDK selection in Azure AI Foundry are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The azure-ai-inference package is the recommended cross-model abstraction over the Model Inference REST API
Switching models with azure-ai-inference can require only changing the model parameter
azure-ai-projects is needed for Foundry-native features such as agents and evaluations
Managed compute autoscaling is configured directly in the Foundry portal deployment blade

questions.explanation: The azure-ai-inference package provides a model-agnostic interface, so only the model parameter changes when switching, while azure-ai-projects unlocks Foundry-native features. Managed compute autoscaling is configured through Azure Machine Learning online endpoint rules, not the Foundry portal.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A RAG application sometimes returns facts that appear in no retrieved document. Which evaluator best measures this problem?

options.text: Fluency
Relevance
Groundedness
Coherence

questions.explanation: Groundedness measures whether each claim in a response is supported by the provided context, so a low score flags fabrication. Relevance measures topical fit, while Fluency and Coherence measure language quality and logical flow.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: For the Northwind Health assistant, an Azure AI Search index must hold embeddings for semantic similarity. What field type must the vector field use?

options.text: Edm.String
Collection(Edm.Single)
Edm.Double
Collection(Edm.String)

questions.explanation: Vector fields in Azure AI Search must be declared as Collection(Edm.Single) to hold the array of single-precision floats that make up an embedding. String or scalar types cannot store a vector.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A team built an orchestration pipeline using Prompt flow. What is the recommended migration target for new development?

options.text: The legacy Completions API
The Microsoft Agent Framework and Responses API
A hand-rolled polling loop over raw REST
The Assistants API only

questions.explanation: Prompt flow is deprecated, and the Microsoft Agent Framework together with the Responses API is its designated replacement for Foundry workflow orchestration. The Completions API is not an orchestration surface.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Prompt flow is deprecated, and new Foundry workflow development should favor the Agent Framework and Responses API rather than Prompt flow.
questions.explanation: Prompt flow is officially deprecated with a scheduled retirement, so production best practice for new workflows is to build on the Agent Framework and the Responses API.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A compliance team must verify that no generated content contains self-harm language. Which evaluator category applies, and at what granularity does it operate?

options.text: A quality evaluator operating on full conversations
A safety evaluator operating on individual turns
An agent evaluator operating on tool calls
A textual similarity evaluator operating on references

questions.explanation: Safety evaluators detect harm categories such as self-harm and operate on individual turns rather than entire conversation threads. Quality and agent evaluators measure different dimensions and are not the correct category for content-safety validation.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about the Foundry Evaluation service are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: AI-assisted quality evaluators require a deployed judge model
Safety evaluators can score a full multi-turn conversation as a single unit
Evaluations can target datasets, model turns, agent runs, or historical traces
Agent evaluators such as Tool Call Success need agent trace data, not just model outputs

questions.explanation: Quality evaluators use a judge model, evaluations can target datasets, models, agents, or traces, and agent evaluators need trace data. Safety evaluators operate on individual turns rather than scoring an entire conversation as one unit.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A developer needs one authenticated session that can both list a Foundry project's connections and obtain an OpenAI-compatible inference client. Which class provides both?

options.text: AzureKeyCredential
AIProjectClient from azure-ai-projects
SearchClient from azure-search-documents
SpeechConfig from the Speech SDK

questions.explanation: AIProjectClient wraps the Foundry project endpoint and exposes sub-clients for connections and inference in a single authenticated session. The other classes are scoped to credentials, search, or speech only.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An agent tool references an external Azure AI Search service, but the deployment fails to reach it. What is a common root cause specific to Foundry?

options.text: The model context window is too small
No project connection is configured for the external service
Streaming was disabled on the client
The judge model was not deployed

questions.explanation: Foundry agents reach external services through configured project connections. A missing connection is a frequent deployment failure because the tool cannot authenticate or query the service without one.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about connecting an application to a Foundry project are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: DefaultAzureCredential selects the right credential across local, CI, and Azure-hosted environments without code changes
Foundry User is the least-privilege role for inference and agent invocation
Full prompt and response content is captured in traces by default
The project endpoint follows the pattern .../api/projects/<project-name>

questions.explanation: DefaultAzureCredential chains through providers automatically, Foundry User is the least-privilege inference role, and the project endpoint uses the .../api/projects/ path. Content recording is disabled by default because prompt and response text may contain PII.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: What is the minimum RBAC role a developer needs to invoke an agent in a Foundry project?

options.text: Foundry Owner
Foundry Account Owner
Foundry User
Contributor at subscription scope

questions.explanation: Foundry User is the least-privilege role that grants inference and agent invocation rights. The Owner and Account Owner roles are for administration and should not be assigned to application developers by default.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which elements make up the definition of a Foundry agent? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: A model selected from the catalog
Instructions defining role, scope, and constraints
A set of tool definitions
A dedicated Azure subscription per agent

questions.explanation: An agent is defined by a model, its instructions, and its tools. Agents do not require a dedicated subscription; they run within a Foundry project.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A developer building a multi-turn chatbot does not want to manually resend the full conversation history on each user message. Which Foundry construct handles this automatically?

options.text: A thread
A run step
A guardrail
A scoring profile

questions.explanation: A thread stores the ordered message history across turns, so appending a new message and starting a run is enough. The Foundry service supplies the full thread context to the model on each run.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An agent run enters requires_action, and the application submits tool outputs 15 minutes after the run started. What happens?

options.text: The outputs are accepted and the run completes
The run fails because tool outputs expire 10 minutes after run creation
The run restarts automatically from the beginning
The outputs queue until the next run

questions.explanation: Tool outputs must be submitted within 10 minutes of run creation. A submission after that window is rejected and the run transitions to a failed state.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An agent must answer questions from a fixed set of PDF documents without the developer writing any retrieval code. Which built-in tool fits?

options.text: Function calling
File search
Code Interpreter
Grounding with Bing Search

questions.explanation: File search automatically indexes uploaded documents and lets the agent retrieve and cite content, requiring no custom retrieval code. Function calling suits dynamic data, and Bing grounding targets live web content.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An agent is configured with an Azure AI Search tool and a valid connection but returns no results despite data being present in the index. What should be verified?

options.text: That the model temperature is above 0.5
That the Foundry project connection name matches the index name exactly
That Code Interpreter is also attached
That streaming is enabled on the client

questions.explanation: The Azure AI Search tool requires the project connection name to match the index name. A mismatch causes silent retrieval failures where the tool appears attached but returns nothing.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A team wants an agent to call an internal REST API described by an OpenAPI 3.0 spec, authenticating with the agent's managed identity. Which tool supports this?

options.text: The Code Interpreter tool
The OpenAPI tool with managed identity authentication
The File Search tool
The Fabric Data Agent

questions.explanation: The OpenAPI tool accepts a 3.0 or 3.1 spec and supports anonymous, API key, and managed identity authentication, letting the agent call external HTTP services securely without stored credentials.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about the Azure AI Search tool for Foundry agents are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Each tool instance supports only one index
It requires a configured Foundry project connection
It supports simple, semantic, vector, and hybrid search types
It can query multiple indexes in a single tool instance

questions.explanation: The Azure AI Search tool binds to one index per instance, requires a project connection, and supports several search types. Querying multiple indexes requires the connected agents pattern with a separate tool per index.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A connected-agents design nests an orchestrator, a subagent, and that subagent's own subagent. When the middle subagent tries to call its child, what happens?

options.text: The run completes normally with three tiers
The run fails with an Assistant Tool Call Depth Error
The child subagent is silently ignored
The orchestrator restarts the run

questions.explanation: The connected agents pattern enforces a maximum depth of 2. A subagent cannot invoke its own subagent, and attempting a third tier produces an Assistant Tool Call Depth Error.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: For the Contoso Travel concierge, a ConnectedAgentTool is created with name set to "flight price bot". What is the result?

options.text: It works because names are free-form
A validation error, because the name may contain only letters and underscores
The spaces are automatically converted to hyphens
The subagent runs but cannot return results

questions.explanation: The name field for a ConnectedAgentTool must contain only letters and underscores. A value with spaces causes a validation error; flight_price_bot would be valid.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: In a connected-agents solution, subagent responses are returned only to the main agent, which synthesizes the final answer for the user.
questions.explanation: Subagents report back to the main orchestrating agent, which combines their outputs into a single cohesive response. End users never interact directly with a subagent.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: The Contoso Travel agent has a tool that emails booking confirmations to customers, and the team wants a human to approve every send. Which tool approval mode should be set?

options.text: never_require
always_require
annotate_only
spotlight

questions.explanation: always_require sends an approval request for every invocation through the human-in-the-loop flow, which is appropriate for outbound, potentially irreversible actions such as customer emails. never_require runs fully autonomously.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An agent keeps invoking the wrong tool for certain requests even though the correct tool exists. Which preview guardrail signal is designed to surface this deviation?

options.text: Spotlighting
Task Adherence
Groundedness Pro
Protected Material detection

questions.explanation: Task Adherence detects misaligned tool invocations, including wrong tool selection, incorrect inputs, and responses inconsistent with user intent, producing a signal to block or escalate. Spotlighting mitigates prompt injection instead.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about safeguards for autonomous Foundry agents are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The AG-UI protocol implements human-in-the-loop approval flows
Spotlighting is a technique to mitigate cross-prompt injection attacks
If no guardrail is explicitly assigned to an agent, the model's default guardrail applies
never_require is the correct mode for irreversible high-risk actions

questions.explanation: AG-UI implements HITL approval, Spotlighting mitigates cross-prompt injection, and the model's default guardrail applies when none is assigned. Irreversible high-risk actions should use always_require, not never_require.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: The Foundry Agent Monitoring Dashboard requires an Application Insights resource to be connected to the project before it can display run telemetry.
questions.explanation: Agent run traces stream to Application Insights, and the monitoring dashboard depends on that connection. Without it, no telemetry is captured and the dashboard has nothing to display.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An agent run has a status of failed. What is the first programmatic step to diagnose the root cause?

options.text: Raise the model temperature and re-run
Inspect run.last_error.message
Delete the thread and start over
Increase the PTU allocation

questions.explanation: The last_error property on a failed run holds the error code and message describing why the run failed. After reading it, trace spans in Application Insights pinpoint the specific tool or model step that failed.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A developer sets temperature to 0.9 and top_p to 0.5 at the same time. Why is this discouraged?

options.text: top_p must always be higher than temperature
Temperature and top_p are alternative randomness controls that should not be tuned simultaneously
The model ignores both parameters when set together
Setting both forces deterministic output

questions.explanation: Temperature and top_p both restrict the token distribution independently, so adjusting both at once produces compounding, unpredictable effects. Best practice is to tune one and leave the other at its default.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An application requires the model to always return output conforming to a specific JSON structure. Which response_format setting enforces schema compliance?

options.text: response_format of type text
response_format of type json_object
response_format of type json_schema
No response_format, relying on prompt wording alone

questions.explanation: A json_schema response format validates the output against a defined schema, guaranteeing the exact shape. The json_object type only guarantees syntactically valid JSON, not a specific structure.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about tuning generation behavior are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The system message is the most impactful lever and should be tuned before numeric parameters
Lower temperature produces more deterministic responses
max_tokens sets a hard upper limit on response length
temperature and top_p should always be adjusted together for best results

questions.explanation: The system message shapes output most strongly, lower temperature increases determinism, and max_tokens caps response length. Temperature and top_p are alternative controls and should not be adjusted simultaneously.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A team wants a model to catch its own arithmetic errors before returning an answer. Which pattern implements this?

options.text: A single call with a higher temperature
A self-critique loop that generates, critiques, then revises
Disabling the system message
Reducing max_tokens to force brevity

questions.explanation: A self-critique loop makes an initial call, a second call to identify errors, and optionally a third to produce the revised answer, catching mistakes before the user sees the response. The other options do not add a review step.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An application must solve advanced mathematical proofs that require deep multi-step reasoning. Which choice is most reliable?

options.text: A standard chat model with a short prompt
An o-series reasoning model such as o3
An embedding model
A small language model for edge devices

questions.explanation: o-series reasoning models perform internal multi-step reasoning without explicit chain-of-thought prompting and are optimized for math, logic, and complex inference. Standard, embedding, and small models are less reliable for advanced proofs.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An operations team needs full prompt and response text captured in distributed traces for debugging. What must they change?

options.text: Increase the model context window
Set AZURE_TRACING_GEN_AI_CONTENT_RECORDING_ENABLED to true
Switch to a Serverless API deployment
Assign the Foundry Account Owner role

questions.explanation: By default traces capture span metadata but not content text, to avoid PII exposure. Setting AZURE_TRACING_GEN_AI_CONTENT_RECORDING_ENABLED to true opts in to recording full prompt and response text.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An application calls a Model Router deployment and needs to log which underlying model handled each request for cost attribution. Where is that information available?

options.text: In a separate billing export only
In the response.model field of the API response
In the request headers sent by the client
It is never exposed to the caller

questions.explanation: The Model Router returns the name of the actual model it selected in the response.model field, which can be logged per turn for cost attribution and debugging.
questions.domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: An application generates images with a gpt-image model and needs to display the result. In what form does the API return the generated image?

options.text: A temporary public URL that expires after 24 hours
Base64-encoded data in the b64_json field
A binary PNG stream with no wrapper
A link to Azure Blob Storage created automatically

questions.explanation: The gpt-image family always returns base64-encoded image data in the b64_json field rather than a hosted URL, so the client decodes the string to obtain the image bytes.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A developer integrates the Sora video generation model. Which interaction pattern is correct?

options.text: A single synchronous call that returns the finished video
Submit a job, poll for completion, then download the result
A streaming WebSocket that emits frames in real time
Upload frames and receive a stitched video immediately

questions.explanation: Video generation is a long-running operation, so the workflow submits a job, polls the job status until it completes, and then downloads the generated video. There is no synchronous single-call response.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A team wants to repaint only a specific region of an existing image while leaving the rest unchanged, using the image edits endpoint. What must the mask satisfy?

options.text: The mask must be a different size to indicate scaling
The mask must match the source image dimensions, with transparent pixels marking the repaint area
The mask must be provided as base64 JSON, not multipart
The mask must cover the whole image with a solid color

questions.explanation: The edits endpoint uses multipart/form-data and a mask whose dimensions match the source image. Transparent pixels in the mask mark the region to repaint while opaque pixels are preserved.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: When submitting a Sora generation job, which parameter most directly drives billing?

options.text: n_variants, the number of candidate clips
n_seconds, the duration of the generated video
The width value only
The output filename

questions.explanation: Video generation is billed primarily by duration, so n_seconds is the main cost driver. Width and height are integer dimensions and n_variants controls how many clips are produced, but duration is the principal billing factor.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about controlling gpt-image generation are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: quality can be set to low, medium, or high
A transparent background is supported with the png output format on gpt-image-1
output_compression applies to jpeg output
Every gpt-image model returns a hosted URL instead of base64

questions.explanation: Generation supports quality levels, transparent backgrounds with png on gpt-image-1, and jpeg output compression. The gpt-image family returns base64 data, not a hosted URL.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: An application must ask a multimodal model open-ended questions about a photo and request high visual fidelity. How is the image passed?

options.text: As a separate Image Analysis REST call before the chat
As an image_url content block in a Chat Completions message, optionally with detail set to high
As a base64 field on the SpeechConfig object
As a query parameter on the deployment URL

questions.explanation: Vision-capable chat models accept images as image_url content blocks within a Chat Completions message, and the detail parameter can be set to high for finer analysis at higher token cost.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A cataloging solution needs a bounding box plus a short description for each distinct region of an image. Which Image Analysis feature fits?

options.text: Caption, which returns one sentence for the whole image
Dense Captions, which returns descriptions with bounding boxes for multiple regions
Tags, which returns labels without coordinates
Read OCR, which extracts text lines

questions.explanation: Dense Captions produces a description and bounding box for several regions of an image. Caption returns a single sentence for the entire image, and Tags returns labels without location data.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: Because the Chat Completions vision endpoint is stateless, a multi-turn visual Q&A app must resend the relevant image and prior turns with each new question.
questions.explanation: The endpoint does not retain conversation state between calls, so the application must include the image and any needed prior context on every request to preserve continuity.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A web team must generate accessible alt text for uploaded photos to satisfy WCAG guidance. Which Image Analysis capability is the direct fit?

options.text: Read OCR
The Caption feature, which returns a concise human-readable description
Smart crop
Background removal

questions.explanation: The Caption feature returns a short natural-language description suitable for alt text, supporting accessibility requirements such as WCAG 1.1.1. OCR, cropping, and background removal do not describe overall image content.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: For Fabrikam Insurance, the team needs a reusable, schema-driven analyzer that extracts fields from embedded photos and reports a confidence value for each field. Which service should they use?

options.text: The Chat Completions vision endpoint with a free-form prompt
Azure AI Content Understanding with a custom analyzer
The Image Analysis Tags feature
The Speech SDK

questions.explanation: Content Understanding lets you define a reusable analyzer with a field schema and can emit per-field confidence and source estimation, which suits repeatable extraction across many similar images. The free-form chat endpoint is not schema-driven or reusable in the same way.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A team uses the Content Understanding prebuilt video analyzer and needs per-shot timing details in the output. What must they set?

options.text: disableFaceBlurring to false
returnDetails to true so cameraShotTimesMs is included
enableSegment to false
The output format to WEBVTT only

questions.explanation: Setting returnDetails to true adds granular metadata such as camera shot times to the analyzer output. WEBVTT is the transcript format and does not by itself include shot timing details.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: Which statements correctly distinguish Azure AI Image Analysis from Content Understanding for visual workloads? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Image Analysis offers prebuilt features such as Caption, Dense Captions, Tags, and OCR
Content Understanding lets you define custom field schemas for extraction
Content Understanding can analyze video and return timestamped segments
Image Analysis is the right choice for schema-driven structured field extraction

questions.explanation: Image Analysis provides fixed prebuilt visual features, while Content Understanding supports custom field schemas and multimodal inputs including video with timestamped segments. Schema-driven structured extraction is a Content Understanding strength, not Image Analysis.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A generative image application must defend against prompt-injection instructions hidden inside user-supplied text. When should Prompt Shields be invoked?

options.text: After the model produces its output, to redact the response
Before the content reaches the model, to detect injection attempts
Only during batch processing
Only on generated images, not text

questions.explanation: Prompt Shields analyze user prompts and any embedded document content for jailbreak and injection attacks, and must run before the content is sent to the model so that a detected attack can be blocked.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about Content Credentials on AI-generated images are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: They are based on the C2PA provenance standard
They are automatically attached to generated images
Removing or altering the image can strip the credential
They are a visible watermark burned into the pixels

questions.explanation: Content Credentials implement the C2PA provenance manifest, are attached automatically to generated images, and can be lost if the image is tampered with. They are cryptographic metadata rather than a visible watermark.
questions.domain_title: Implement computer vision solutions

questions.stem: A team must extract organization-specific entity types that are not part of any built-in taxonomy, such as internal part numbers. Which capability should they use?

options.text: Prebuilt Named Entity Recognition
Custom Named Entity Recognition trained on labeled data
Key phrase extraction
Language detection

questions.explanation: Prebuilt NER recognizes only a fixed taxonomy of general categories. Custom NER lets you label examples of your own entity types and train a model to extract them.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A summarization feature must produce a novel, rephrased summary rather than reusing verbatim sentences from the source. Which mode is required?

options.text: Extractive summarization
Abstractive summarization
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis

questions.explanation: Extractive summarization selects and ranks existing sentences, while abstractive summarization generates new wording that paraphrases the content. Only abstractive mode produces novel text.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A product team wants sentiment broken down by the specific aspect being discussed, such as "battery" versus "screen" in a review. What must be enabled?

options.text: Language detection
Opinion mining, by setting the show_opinion_mining flag
PII redaction
Extractive summarization

questions.explanation: Opinion mining, enabled with the show_opinion_mining flag, returns aspect (target) terms and the sentiment expressed toward each one, giving aspect-level rather than document-level sentiment.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about the PII detection and redaction feature are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: It returns a redacted_text field with detected entities masked
It can run in a Docker container for on-premises processing
It supports text, conversation, and document input shapes
It always uploads documents to a public endpoint with no container option

questions.explanation: PII detection returns redacted_text with entities masked, offers a container image for on-premises use, and supports text, conversation, and document inputs. The container option specifically avoids sending data to a cloud endpoint.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A translation workload processes millions of short strings and wants the most cost-effective option billed on character count. Which choice fits?

options.text: LLM-based translation billed per token
Neural Machine Translation through the Translator service, billed per character
The Speech translation SDK
Transliteration

questions.explanation: Translator's Neural Machine Translation is billed by characters and supports large per-request batches, making it economical for high-volume short strings. LLM-based translation is billed per token and has smaller request limits.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: Transliteration converts text from one writing script to another while keeping the same language, rather than translating it into a different language.
questions.explanation: Transliteration maps characters between scripts, for example rendering Japanese text in Latin characters, without changing the underlying language. Translation, by contrast, changes the language itself.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: Which capability-to-task mappings for the Azure AI Language and Translator services are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Named Entity Recognition to identify people, places, and organizations
Summarization to condense long documents
Translator to convert text into another language
Sentiment analysis to generate images from text

questions.explanation: NER identifies entities, summarization condenses text, and Translator converts between languages. Sentiment analysis scores emotional tone and does not generate images.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A desktop utility must transcribe a single short spoken command from the microphone using the Speech SDK. Which combination is correct?

options.text: A SpeechSynthesizer calling speak_text_async
A SpeechRecognizer configured with SpeechConfig and AudioConfig, calling recognize_once_async
The batch transcription REST API
A TranslationRecognizer with two target languages

questions.explanation: Real-time single-utterance transcription uses a SpeechRecognizer built from a SpeechConfig and an AudioConfig, invoking recognize_once_async. SpeechSynthesizer performs text-to-speech, and batch transcription is for large offline files.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: Fabrikam Insurance must transcribe thousands of pre-recorded audio tracks pulled from claim video clips, running asynchronously overnight. Which approach is appropriate?

options.text: Real-time recognize_once_async in a loop
The batch transcription REST API
A SpeechSynthesizer
The text Translator service

questions.explanation: Batch transcription is a REST-based asynchronous service designed for large volumes of stored audio files, which fits an overnight bulk job. Real-time recognition is meant for live, interactive input.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A hospital wants higher recognition accuracy on specialized medical terms. After training a Custom Speech model, what is required to use it for real-time recognition?

options.text: Nothing; custom models apply automatically to the base endpoint
Deploy the model to a custom endpoint and reference its endpoint_id
Switch to the Translator service
Enable opinion mining

questions.explanation: A trained Custom Speech model must be deployed to a dedicated endpoint, and the application references that endpoint_id for real-time recognition. Word Error Rate is used to evaluate improvement before deployment.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A developer sends audio to a gpt-4o-audio-preview model using an input_audio content block and also wants the response validated against a JSON schema. What is the limitation?

options.text: input_audio blocks are not supported by audio models
Structured Outputs with json_schema is not supported for audio-preview models
Audio models cannot return any text
The audio must first be transcribed by Translator

questions.explanation: Audio-preview models accept input_audio content but do not support the Structured Outputs json_schema response format, so schema-validated output cannot be combined with audio input on those models.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about real-time and LLM-based speech options are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The GPT Realtime API uses a WebSocket connection for low-latency streaming
LLM Speech offers a multi-lingual default that recognizes across many languages
Some real-time translation models are billed hourly
Structured Outputs is guaranteed for every audio-preview model

questions.explanation: The GPT Realtime API streams over WebSocket, LLM Speech provides a multi-lingual default, and certain real-time translation models bill hourly. Structured Outputs is not supported on audio-preview models.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: Using the Speech SDK for speech translation, a caller sets three target languages in a single request. What happens?

options.text: All three translations are returned normally
A single call is limited to two target languages; more requires a multi-service Foundry resource
Only transliteration is returned
The source language is auto-detected and the targets are ignored

questions.explanation: A standard speech translation call supports up to two target languages at once. Translating into more targets simultaneously requires a multi-service Foundry resource configuration.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A conference app must translate speech when the spoken source language is unknown and may change between speakers. Which capability fits?

options.text: A fixed source language on SpeechTranslationConfig
Live Interpreter, which auto-detects the source language
Transliteration
Extractive summarization

questions.explanation: Live Interpreter automatically detects the spoken source language, which suits multi-speaker sessions where the language is not known in advance. A fixed source language would fail when speakers switch languages.
questions.domain_title: Implement text analysis solutions

questions.stem: A team configures an Azure AI Search indexer to pull content from Blob Storage. Which statement about the indexer pipeline is correct?

options.text: One indexer can write to multiple indexes from multiple data sources at once
An indexer connects one data source to one index and runs stages such as document cracking, field mappings, skillset, and output field mappings
Indexers can only run on demand and never on a schedule
Indexers require the push API to load documents

questions.explanation: An indexer binds a single data source to a single index and runs a defined sequence of stages including document cracking, field mappings, optional skillset enrichment, and output field mappings. It is the pull model, distinct from the push API.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A skillset must run OCR over images embedded in PDFs, but no normalized images are being produced for the OCR skill to consume. What is the likely cause?

options.text: The index has too few replicas
imageAction is left at its default of none, so normalized images are not generated
Semantic ranking is disabled
The schedule interval is below five minutes

questions.explanation: Image extraction is off by default. To feed OCR, the indexer configuration must set imageAction to generateNormalizedImages so that embedded images are extracted and normalized before the skillset runs.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A large index must serve low-latency approximate nearest-neighbor vector search. Which algorithm should be configured?

options.text: Exhaustive KNN over the entire index
HNSW
BM25 keyword ranking
Semantic ranking only

questions.explanation: HNSW provides fast approximate nearest-neighbor search that scales to large indexes with low latency. Exhaustive KNN gives exact results but is slower and does not scale as well for large collections.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about hybrid search and semantic ranking in Azure AI Search are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Hybrid search fuses keyword and vector results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion
The semantic ranker re-scores a top subset of candidates
Semantic ranking requires a semantic configuration on the index
Semantic ranking replaces the need for any vector field

questions.explanation: Hybrid search combines BM25 and vector results with RRF, the semantic ranker re-scores the top candidates, and a semantic configuration must be defined on the index. Semantic ranking complements rather than replaces vector fields.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about skillsets in Azure AI Search are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Built-in skills include OCR and text splitting
A WebApiSkill can call custom logic hosted as a web service
A skillset can write enriched output to a knowledge store in addition to the index
Skillsets can only run without an indexer

questions.explanation: Skillsets provide built-in skills such as OCR and text splitting, support custom WebApiSkill calls, and can project enrichments to a knowledge store as well as the index. Skillsets run as part of an indexer pipeline.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: For the Northwind Health index, long policy PDFs must be split into overlapping chunks before embedding. Which built-in skill and setting control the chunk size?

options.text: The OCR skill with the detail parameter
The Text Split skill using maximumPageLength and pageOverlapLength
The Sentiment skill
The Entity Recognition skill

questions.explanation: The Text Split skill divides content into pages or sentences, with maximumPageLength setting chunk size and pageOverlapLength adding overlap between chunks so context is not lost at boundaries. Overlap is typically 10 to 25 percent.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A team maps each parent document to many child chunk records so that retrieval returns fine-grained passages traceable to their source document. Which integrated-vectorization feature enables this?

options.text: Scoring profiles
Index projections that create a parent-child relationship
Synonym maps
Analyzer overrides

questions.explanation: Index projections split an enriched parent document into multiple child records, one per chunk, while preserving the link to the parent. This is how chunked RAG indexes retain document lineage.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A solution needs a retrieval step that decomposes a complex multi-part question into subqueries, runs them, and merges the results. Which Azure AI Search capability provides this?

options.text: Simple keyword search
Agentic retrieval using a knowledge base with an LLM query planner
A scoring profile
A synonym map

questions.explanation: Agentic retrieval uses an LLM to plan and decompose a complex query into subqueries against a knowledge base, then merges the results. It requires a semantic configuration and is the foundation for query planning in search.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: Agentic retrieval provides the retrieval foundation for Foundry IQ and is billed based on token usage in addition to search operations.
questions.explanation: Agentic retrieval underlies Foundry IQ and, because it invokes an LLM for query planning, incurs token-based billing in addition to standard search costs.

options.text: True
False

questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: Fabrikam Insurance must extract a handful of custom fields from claim forms without collecting labeled training data. Which Document Intelligence capability fits?

options.text: Training a fully custom neural model
The layout model with query field extraction
The prebuilt receipt model only
The Read model with sentiment

questions.explanation: The layout model supports query field extraction, which pulls specified fields on demand without any labeling or training. Building a fully custom model would require assembling and labeling training documents.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A RAG pipeline needs clean structural output from scanned documents, including tables and headings rendered as Markdown. Which Document Intelligence model produces this?

options.text: The prebuilt business card model
The layout model, which can emit Markdown output
The Read model with translation
A custom classification model

questions.explanation: The layout model extracts text, tables, and structure and can output Markdown, which preserves headings and tables for downstream RAG chunking. It also handles printed and handwritten text across many languages.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A team wants a single service that returns clean Markdown, describes embedded charts and figures, and includes grounding such as bounding boxes and confidence for a RAG index. Which service should they choose?

options.text: Azure AI Content Understanding
The Translator service
The Speech SDK
The prebuilt sentiment model

questions.explanation: Content Understanding produces clean Markdown, generates descriptions for figures and charts, and attaches grounding such as bounding boxes, timestamps, and confidence scores, making it well suited to preparing multimodal content for RAG.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: Which statements about Azure AI Content Understanding analyzers are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Content extraction analyzers such as prebuilt-read and prebuilt-layout do not use a generative model
Of the Extract, Classify, and Generate methods, only Generate uses an LLM
A copy operation can freeze a prebuilt analyzer so its behavior does not change
Every analyzer category always invokes a generative model

questions.explanation: Content extraction analyzers do not call a generative model, only the Generate method uses an LLM, and copying an analyzer pins its behavior. Not every category uses generative AI, which is why content extraction is available without it.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions

questions.stem: A team analyzes long recordings with Content Understanding and wants segments that align to topic or scene boundaries rather than fixed time intervals. What is true about segmentation?

options.text: Audio and video are always split into fixed-length intervals only
Audio and video can be segmented by topic or scene rather than by a fixed interval
Segmentation is not supported for audio or video
Only the Translator service can segment media

questions.explanation: Content Understanding can segment audio and video by semantic boundaries such as topic or scene changes, which produces more meaningful units than fixed-interval slicing.
questions.domain_title: Implement information extraction solutions
